TEXT.description: The built-in immutable string type in Modula-3, representing a sequence of characters with reference semantics. TEXT values are garbage-collected and support concatenation with the ampersand operator.

Length.description: Returns the number of characters in the given text value.

GetChar.description: Returns the character at position i in the text, with zero-based indexing.

Sub.signature: Text.Sub(t: TEXT, start: CARDINAL, length: CARDINAL) -> TEXT
Sub.description: Returns a substring of t starting at position start with the given length.

Equal.description: Returns TRUE if the two text values contain identical character sequences.

Cat.description: Concatenates two text values and returns the combined result.

INTEGER.description: The built-in signed integer type in Modula-3, representing whole numbers within the platform-dependent range. Supports standard arithmetic, comparison, and bitwise operations defined by the language.

REAL.description: The standard single-precision floating-point type in Modula-3, conforming to IEEE 754 representation. Used for numeric computations requiring fractional values with moderate precision.

LONGREAL.description: The double-precision floating-point type in Modula-3, providing higher precision than REAL for scientific and mathematical computations requiring greater accuracy in decimal representation.

BOOLEAN.description: The built-in enumeration type with exactly two values, FALSE and TRUE, used for logical operations, conditionals, and control flow in Modula-3 programs.

CHAR.description: The built-in character type representing a single 8-bit character in the ISO Latin-1 character set. CHAR values are ordinal and support ORD and VAL operations for conversion.

ARRAY.description: A fixed-size or open array type in Modula-3 that stores elements of a uniform type indexed by an ordinal type. Open arrays use ARRAY OF T syntax and are passed by reference.

NUMBER.description: Returns the number of elements in the array.

FIRST.description: Returns the first valid index of the array.

LAST.description: Returns the last valid index of the array.

RECORD.signature: RECORD field1: Type1; field2: Type2; END
RECORD.description: A composite value type in Modula-3 that groups named fields of potentially different types into a single structured data unit, similar to structs in C or records in Pascal.

NEW.description: Allocates heap memory for a reference type and returns a traced reference to the newly created object. Accepts optional field initializers for record and object types in Modula-3.

THREAD.description: The standard Modula-3 interface for concurrent programming, providing thread creation via Fork, synchronization with Mutex and Condition variables, and cooperative scheduling primitives.

Fork.description: Creates and starts a new thread executing the given closure's apply method.

Join.description: Blocks the calling thread until thread t completes and returns its result.

Wait.description: Atomically releases the mutex and suspends the thread until the condition is signaled.

Signal.description: Wakes one thread waiting on the given condition variable.

Broadcast.description: Wakes all threads waiting on the given condition variable.

Fmt.description: The standard formatting interface in Modula-3 providing functions to convert integers, reals, booleans, and characters to their TEXT representations for output and string construction.

Int.description: Converts an integer to its text representation in the specified base.

Real.signature: Fmt.Real(x: REAL, style: Fmt.Style, prec: CARDINAL) -> TEXT
Real.description: Converts a real number to text with the given formatting style and precision.

Bool.description: Returns the text TRUE or FALSE for the given boolean value.

Char.description: Converts a single character to a one-character text value.

Wr.description: The standard writer interface in Modula-3 providing buffered output streams for writing text to files, standard output, and other destinations with flush and close operations.

PutText.description: Writes the given text string to the output writer stream.

PutChar.description: Writes a single character to the output writer stream.

Flush.description: Flushes any buffered output to the underlying destination.

Close.description: Flushes remaining output and closes the writer, releasing associated resources.

Rd.description: The standard reader interface in Modula-3 providing buffered input streams for reading characters and lines from files, standard input, and other sources with EOF detection.

GetChar.description: Reads and returns the next character from the input reader stream.

GetLine.description: Reads and returns text up to the next newline character from the input stream.

EOF.description: Returns TRUE if the reader has reached the end of the input stream.

Close.description: Closes the reader and releases associated resources.
